title: 5-stage personality decision filter
acceptance_criteria: Export a pure function runPersonalityFilter(signal, personality, dailyState, currentVix, clock) that returns {passed: true} or {passed: false, stage: 1|2|3|4|5, reason: string}

Stage 1 — Hard filters (pure, synchronous): (a) signal.signal_type matches personality's accepted signal types (Clockwork/Learners accept 'SCHEDULED'; momentum personalities accept 'MOMENTUM_EXHAUSTION'|'PULLBACK'; Scanner/Blitz also accept 'SCHEDULED' as fallback); (b) signal time is within trading hours (ENTRY_START_TIME..ENTRY_CUTOFF_TIME from env); (c) today's date is not in blocked_dates

Stage 2 — State checks (uses pre-fetched DailyState): (a) dailyState.tradeCount < personality.params.max_daily_trades (or Infinity if not set); (b) dailyState.netPnl > -personality.params.max_daily_loss; (c) dailyState.openPositions < (personality.params.max_open_legs ?? 2) / 2 (i.e. open straddle count check)

Stage 3 — Context checks (uses currentVix): (a) if personality.params.vix_max is set, currentVix must be <= vix_max; (b) regime check is SKIPPED in Phase 1 (no regime tagging yet — documented as deferred to T-33 in M4)
Stage 4 — Signal quality (pure): signal.adjusted_probability >= (personality.params.min_probability ?? 0) — passes for Clockwork/Learners (min_probability not set, defaults to 0, so always passes)

Stage 5 — Profit gate (optional, uses pre-fetched DailyState): if personality.params.require_profit_gate is true, pass only if dailyState.netPnl < personality.params.profit_gate_amount (default: gate disabled/null means always pass)
DailyState interface exported: { tradeCount: number; netPnl: string; openPositions: number }

fetchDailyState(db, personalityId, todayIST) exported: single DB query that fetches trade count, sum of net_pnl, and count of open positions for a given personality+date — used by T-27 PersonalityRouter to pre-fetch before calling runPersonalityFilter

Unit tests cover each stage independently: stage 1 blocks on wrong signal_type, wrong time, blocked date; stage 2 blocks on too many trades, daily loss hit, too many open positions; stage 3 blocks on VIX ceiling; stage 4 blocks on low probability; stage 5 blocks when profit gate active and already profitable
Every stage is independently testable by passing controlled inputs — no async in the core filter function itself

checkComparisonIntegrity(configs: PersonalityConfig[]) function exported: checks that Precision, Adjuster, Reducer min_probability values are within 8 percentage points of each other; returns {valid: boolean, offender?: string, message?: string}
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
